title: Polynomial-time solvability of totally $\Delta$-modular integer programs
statement_text: Can integer programs defined by totally $\Delta$-modular matrices be solved in polynomial time whenever $\Delta$ is a constant?
context_text: Artmann, Weismantel, and Zenklusen [2] showed that totally 2-modular IPs are solvable in strongly polynomial time, extending the classical totally unimodular case. In view of further work on programs with bounded subdeterminants [42, 44, 4, 12, 14, 34], the question of whether polynomial-time solvability extends to every fixed $\Delta$ is natural but remains open even for $\Delta = 3$.
paper_contribution: Proves a strongly polynomial-time algorithm for the special case where the totally $\Delta$-modular matrix $A$ contains at most two nonzero entries in each row or in each column (Theorem 1), thereby settling this restricted case for all constant $\Delta$.
notes: No specific author attribution is given in the paper; stated in introduction prose as 'still open (even for $\Delta=3$)'. Statement text is a faithful paraphrase of the prose since no formal environment exists.
paper_title: Integer programs with bounded subdeterminants and two nonzeros per row
